headline: Taman Negara Visitor Numbers Triple Park's Carrying Capacity
context: DWNP data shows Taman Negara received 420,000 visitors in 2024, approximately three times the ecologically sustainable carrying capacity of 140,000 estimated by a 2020 UKM study.

cards.big: Taman Negara can sustain 140,000 visitors per year without ecological damage.
cards.sub: 420,000 visited in 2024. Trail erosion, wildlife displacement, and waste pollution are accelerating.

cards.big: Canopy walkway trail erosion has deepened 40cm since 2018.
cards.sub: Root exposure from foot traffic is killing mature trees along the park's most iconic feature.

cards.big: Park entry fee is RM1 for Malaysians — unchanged since 1989.
cards.sub: Total fee revenue covers 12% of park management costs. The rest comes from federal allocation.

cards.big: Bhutan limits visitors and charges USD 200 per day to fund conservation.
cards.sub: Malaysia's approach maximises volume at minimal price — the opposite of sustainable tourism.

cards.big: Promoting a national park to 3 times its capacity while charging RM1 entry is loving it to death.
Visitor caps and realistic pricing are not anti-tourism. They are pro-park. Without them, Taman Negara's appeal will be destroyed by its own popularity.
